{{Infobox ancient site | name = Urama Chausuyama Kofun | native_name =浦間茶臼山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Urama Chausuyama Kofun air.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Aerial photo of Urama Chausuyama Kofun in 1980 | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Urama Chausuyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|42|36.2|N|134|4|1.3|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Higashi-ku, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid 3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|42|36.2|N|134|4|1.3|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Urama Chausuyama Kofun'''|浦間茶臼山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Urama neighborhood of [[Higashi-ku, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/172965|title=浦間茶臼山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is believed to have been built at the end of the 3rd century in the early Kofun period. It is one of the oldest large keyhole-shaped burial mounds in ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]].
==Overview== The Urama Chausuyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above. It is located on a small hill between the [[Yoshii River]] and the Sunagawa River flowing through eastern Okayama Prefecture. The tumulus, which is orientated to the east, has a total length of about 138 meters, with its circular posterior portion having a diameter of about 81 meters and a height of about 13.8 meters. The anterior rectangular portion, which is about 61 meters wide at the tip, spreads out in the shape of a [[shamisen]] [[plectrum]]. The posterior circular part is built in three stages and has remnants of [[breccia]] ''[[fukiishi]]'' and shards of trapezoidal-size cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. However, the tumulus has been much transformed from its original form, and its original scale is a matter of speculation. The anterior part was cultivated as a field, and now it is a park with [[sakura|cherry trees]]. The posterior portion has a bamboo grove and a cemetery on its north side. Residential complexes are located on the east and south sides of the burial mound. It is believed to have originally been a half-scale replica of the [[Hashihaka Kofun]] in [[Sakurai, Nara]] with which it is contemporary in age.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In 1988, an [[archaeological excavation]] confirmed a vertical pit-type stone [[burial chamber]] in the posterior porton of the tumulus, which was constructed by slabs of [[andesite]] which had been quarried in what is now northern [[Kagawa Prefecture]] or one of the islands in the [[Seto Inland Sea]]. The floor of the burial camber was covered with clay containing red pigment, and it is presumed that a split bamboo-shaped wooden coffin was placed inside. However, the burial chamber had been robbed during the [[Meiji period]] and most of the [[grave goods]] have been lost. The 1988 excavation did find fragments of a [[bronze mirror]], bronze and iron arrowheads, iron swords, spears and axes, and agricultural tools such as sickles and spades.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> File:Urama Chausuyama Kofun 07.jpg|Panoramic view File:Urama Chausuyama Kofun 03.jpg|Anterior Portion File:Urama Chausuyama Kofun 04.jpg|Posterior Portion File:Urama Chausuyama Kofun 06.jpg|Site of Burial Chamber </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:|Urama Chausuyama Kofun}} *https://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/01.html Okayama City home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.okayama.jp/site/kodai/632635.html Okayama Prefecture home page]{{in lang|ja}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Urama Chausuyama Kofun | native_name =浦間茶臼山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Urama Chausuyama Kofun air.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Aerial photo of Urama Chausuyama Kofun in 1980 | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Urama Chausuyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|42|36.2|N|134|4|1.3|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Higashi-ku, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid 3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|42|36.2|N|134|4|1.3|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Urama Chausuyama Kofun'''|浦間茶臼山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Urama neighborhood of [[Higashi-ku, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/172965|title=浦間茶臼山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is believed to have been built at the end of the 3rd century in the early Kofun period. It is one of the oldest large keyhole-shaped burial mounds in ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]].
==Overview== The Urama Chausuyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above. It is located on a small hill between the [[Yoshii River]] and the Sunagawa River flowing through eastern Okayama Prefecture. The tumulus, which is orientated to the east, has a total length of about 138 meters, with its circular posterior portion having a diameter of about 81 meters and a height of about 13.8 meters. The anterior rectangular portion, which is about 61 meters wide at the tip, spreads out in the shape of a [[shamisen]] [[plectrum]]. The posterior circular part is built in three stages and has remnants of [[breccia]] ''[[fukiishi]]'' and shards of trapezoidal-size cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. However, the tumulus has been much transformed from its original form, and its original scale is a matter of speculation. The anterior part was cultivated as a field, and now it is a park with [[sakura|cherry trees]]. The posterior portion has a bamboo grove and a cemetery on its north side. Residential complexes are located on the east and south sides of the burial mound. It is believed to have originally been a half-scale replica of the [[Hashihaka Kofun]] in [[Sakurai, Nara]] with which it is contemporary in age.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In 1988, an [[archaeological excavation]] confirmed a vertical pit-type stone [[burial chamber]] in the posterior porton of the tumulus, which was constructed by slabs of [[andesite]] which had been quarried in what is now northern [[Kagawa Prefecture]] or one of the islands in the [[Seto Inland Sea]]. The floor of the burial camber was covered with clay containing red pigment, and it is presumed that a split bamboo-shaped wooden coffin was placed inside. However, the burial chamber had been robbed during the [[Meiji period]] and most of the [[grave goods]] have been lost. The 1988 excavation did find fragments of a [[bronze mirror]], bronze and iron arrowheads, iron swords, spears and axes, and agricultural tools such as sickles and spades.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> File:Urama Chausuyama Kofun 07.jpg|Panoramic view File:Urama Chausuyama Kofun 03.jpg|Anterior Portion File:Urama Chausuyama Kofun 04.jpg|Posterior Portion File:Urama Chausuyama Kofun 06.jpg|Site of Burial Chamber </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Urama Chausuyama Kofun | native_name =浦間茶臼山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Urama Chausuyama Kofun air.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Aerial photo of Urama Chausuyama Kofun in 1980 | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Urama Chausuyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|42|36.2|N|134|4|1.3|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Higashi-ku, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid 3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|42|36.2|N|134|4|1.3|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Urama Chausuyama Kofun'''|浦間茶臼山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Urama neighborhood of [[Higashi-ku, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/172965|title=浦間茶臼山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is believed to have been built at the end of the 3rd century in the early Kofun period. It is one of the oldest large keyhole-shaped burial mounds in ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]].
==Overview== The Urama Chausuyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above. It is located on a small hill between the [[Yoshii River]] and the Sunagawa River flowing through eastern Okayama Prefecture. The tumulus, which is orientated to the east, has a total length of about 138 meters, with its circular posterior portion having a diameter of about 81 meters and a height of about 13.8 meters. The anterior rectangular portion, which is about 61 meters wide at the tip, spreads out in the shape of a [[shamisen]] [[plectrum]]. The posterior circular part is built in three stages and has remnants of [[breccia]] ''[[fukiishi]]'' and shards of trapezoidal-size cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. However, the tumulus has been much transformed from its original form, and its original scale is a matter of speculation. The anterior part was cultivated as a field, and now it is a park with [[sakura|cherry trees]]. The posterior portion has a bamboo grove and a cemetery on its north side. Residential complexes are located on the east and south sides of the burial mound. It is believed to have originally been a half-scale replica of the [[Hashihaka Kofun]] in [[Sakurai, Nara]] with which it is contemporary in age.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In 1988, an [[archaeological excavation]] confirmed a vertical pit-type stone [[burial chamber]] in the posterior porton of the tumulus, which was constructed by slabs of [[andesite]] which had been quarried in what is now northern [[Kagawa Prefecture]] or one of the islands in the [[Seto Inland Sea]]. The floor of the burial camber was covered with clay containing red pigment, and it is presumed that a split bamboo-shaped wooden coffin was placed inside. However, the burial chamber had been robbed during the [[Meiji period]] and most of the [[grave goods]] have been lost. The 1988 excavation did find fragments of a [[bronze mirror]], bronze and iron arrowheads, iron swords, spears and axes, and agricultural tools such as sickles and spades.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

However, the tumulus has been much transformed from its original form, and its original scale is a matter of speculation. The anterior part was cultivated as a field, and now it is a park with [[sakura|cherry trees]]. The posterior portion has a bamboo grove and a cemetery on its north side. Residential complexes are located on the east and south sides of the burial mound. It is believed to have originally been a half-scale replica of the [[Hashihaka Kofun]] in [[Sakurai, Nara]] with which it is contemporary in age.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In 1988, an [[archaeological excavation]] confirmed a vertical pit-type stone [[burial chamber]] in the posterior porton of the tumulus, which was constructed by slabs of [[andesite]] which had been quarried in what is now northern [[Kagawa Prefecture]] or one of the islands in the [[Seto Inland Sea]]. The floor of the burial camber was covered with clay containing red pigment, and it is presumed that a split bamboo-shaped wooden coffin was placed inside. However, the burial chamber had been robbed during the [[Meiji period]] and most of the [[grave goods]] have been lost. The 1988 excavation did find fragments of a [[bronze mirror]], bronze and iron arrowheads, iron swords, spears and axes, and agricultural tools such as sickles and spades.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{nihongo|'''Urama Chausuyama Kofun'''|浦間茶臼山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Urama neighborhood of [[Higashi-ku, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/172965|title=浦間茶臼山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is believed to have been built at the end of the 3rd century in the early Kofun period. It is one of the oldest large keyhole-shaped burial mounds in ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]].
==Overview== The Urama Chausuyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above. It is located on a small hill between the [[Yoshii River]] and the Sunagawa River flowing through eastern Okayama Prefecture. The tumulus, which is orientated to the east, has a total length of about 138 meters, with its circular posterior portion having a diameter of about 81 meters and a height of about 13.8 meters. The anterior rectangular portion, which is about 61 meters wide at the tip, spreads out in the shape of a [[shamisen]] [[plectrum]]. The posterior circular part is built in three stages and has remnants of [[breccia]] ''[[fukiishi]]'' and shards of trapezoidal-size cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. However, the tumulus has been much transformed from its original form, and its original scale is a matter of speculation. The anterior part was cultivated as a field, and now it is a park with [[sakura|cherry trees]]. The posterior portion has a bamboo grove and a cemetery on its north side. Residential complexes are located on the east and south sides of the burial mound. It is believed to have originally been a half-scale replica of the [[Hashihaka Kofun]] in [[Sakurai, Nara]] with which it is contemporary in age.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In 1988, an [[archaeological excavation]] confirmed a vertical pit-type stone [[burial chamber]] in the posterior porton of the tumulus, which was constructed by slabs of [[andesite]] which had been quarried in what is now northern [[Kagawa Prefecture]] or one of the islands in the [[Seto Inland Sea]]. The floor of the burial camber was covered with clay containing red pigment, and it is presumed that a split bamboo-shaped wooden coffin was placed inside. However, the burial chamber had been robbed during the [[Meiji period]] and most of the [[grave goods]] have been lost. The 1988 excavation did find fragments of a [[bronze mirror]], bronze and iron arrowheads, iron swords, spears and axes, and agricultural tools such as sickles and spades.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Urama Chausuyama Kofun | native_name =浦間茶臼山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Urama Chausuyama Kofun air.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Aerial photo of Urama Chausuyama Kofun in 1980 | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Urama Chausuyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|42|36.2|N|134|4|1.3|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Higashi-ku, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid 3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|42|36.2|N|134|4|1.3|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Urama Chausuyama Kofun'''|浦間茶臼山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Urama neighborhood of [[Higashi-ku, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/172965|title=浦間茶臼山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is believed to have been built at the end of the 3rd century in the early Kofun period. It is one of the oldest large keyhole-shaped burial mounds in ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]].
==Overview== The Urama Chausuyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above. It is located on a small hill between the [[Yoshii River]] and the Sunagawa River flowing through eastern Okayama Prefecture. The tumulus, which is orientated to the east, has a total length of about 138 meters, with its circular posterior portion having a diameter of about 81 meters and a height of about 13.8 meters. The anterior rectangular portion, which is about 61 meters wide at the tip, spreads out in the shape of a [[shamisen]] [[plectrum]]. The posterior circular part is built in three stages and has remnants of [[breccia]] ''[[fukiishi]]'' and shards of trapezoidal-size cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. However, the tumulus has been much transformed from its original form, and its original scale is a matter of speculation. The anterior part was cultivated as a field, and now it is a park with [[sakura|cherry trees]]. The posterior portion has a bamboo grove and a cemetery on its north side. Residential complexes are located on the east and south sides of the burial mound. It is believed to have originally been a half-scale replica of the [[Hashihaka Kofun]] in [[Sakurai, Nara]] with which it is contemporary in age.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In 1988, an [[archaeological excavation]] confirmed a vertical pit-type stone [[burial chamber]] in the posterior porton of the tumulus, which was constructed by slabs of [[andesite]] which had been quarried in what is now northern [[Kagawa Prefecture]] or one of the islands in the [[Seto Inland Sea]]. The floor of the burial camber was covered with clay containing red pigment, and it is presumed that a split bamboo-shaped wooden coffin was placed inside. However, the burial chamber had been robbed during the [[Meiji period]] and most of the [[grave goods]] have been lost. The 1988 excavation did find fragments of a [[bronze mirror]], bronze and iron arrowheads, iron swords, spears and axes, and agricultural tools such as sickles and spades.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> File:Urama Chausuyama Kofun 07.jpg|Panoramic view File:Urama Chausuyama Kofun 03.jpg|Anterior Portion File:Urama Chausuyama Kofun 04.jpg|Posterior Portion File:Urama Chausuyama Kofun 06.jpg|Site of Burial Chamber </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Urama Chausuyama Kofun | native_name =浦間茶臼山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Urama Chausuyama Kofun air.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Aerial photo of Urama Chausuyama Kofun in 1980 | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Urama Chausuyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|42|36.2|N|134|4|1.3|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Higashi-ku, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid 3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|42|36.2|N|134|4|1.3|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Urama Chausuyama Kofun'''|浦間茶臼山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Urama neighborhood of [[Higashi-ku, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/172965|title=浦間茶臼山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is believed to have been built at the end of the 3rd century in the early Kofun period. It is one of the oldest large keyhole-shaped burial mounds in ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]].
==Overview== The Urama Chausuyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above. It is located on a small hill between the [[Yoshii River]] and the Sunagawa River flowing through eastern Okayama Prefecture. The tumulus, which is orientated to the east, has a total length of about 138 meters, with its circular posterior portion having a diameter of about 81 meters and a height of about 13.8 meters. The anterior rectangular portion, which is about 61 meters wide at the tip, spreads out in the shape of a [[shamisen]] [[plectrum]]. The posterior circular part is built in three stages and has remnants of [[breccia]] ''[[fukiishi]]'' and shards of trapezoidal-size cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. However, the tumulus has been much transformed from its original form, and its original scale is a matter of speculation. The anterior part was cultivated as a field, and now it is a park with [[sakura|cherry trees]]. The posterior portion has a bamboo grove and a cemetery on its north side. Residential complexes are located on the east and south sides of the burial mound. It is believed to have originally been a half-scale replica of the [[Hashihaka Kofun]] in [[Sakurai, Nara]] with which it is contemporary in age.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In 1988, an [[archaeological excavation]] confirmed a vertical pit-type stone [[burial chamber]] in the posterior porton of the tumulus, which was constructed by slabs of [[andesite]] which had been quarried in what is now northern [[Kagawa Prefecture]] or one of the islands in the [[Seto Inland Sea]]. The floor of the burial camber was covered with clay containing red pigment, and it is presumed that a split bamboo-shaped wooden coffin was placed inside. However, the burial chamber had been robbed during the [[Meiji period]] and most of the [[grave goods]] have been lost. The 1988 excavation did find fragments of a [[bronze mirror]], bronze and iron arrowheads, iron swords, spears and axes, and agricultural tools such as sickles and spades.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> File:Urama Chausuyama Kofun 07.jpg|Panoramic view File:Urama Chausuyama Kofun 03.jpg|Anterior Portion File:Urama Chausuyama Kofun 04.jpg|Posterior Portion File:Urama Chausuyama Kofun 06.jpg|Site of Burial Chamber </gallery>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Urama Chausuyama Kofun | native_name =浦間茶臼山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Urama Chausuyama Kofun air.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Aerial photo of Urama Chausuyama Kofun in 1980 | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Urama Chausuyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|42|36.2|N|134|4|1.3|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Higashi-ku, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid 3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|42|36.2|N|134|4|1.3|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Urama Chausuyama Kofun'''|浦間茶臼山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Urama neighborhood of [[Higashi-ku, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/172965|title=浦間茶臼山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is believed to have been built at the end of the 3rd century in the early Kofun period. It is one of the oldest large keyhole-shaped burial mounds in ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]].
==Overview== The Urama Chausuyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above. It is located on a small hill between the [[Yoshii River]] and the Sunagawa River flowing through eastern Okayama Prefecture. The tumulus, which is orientated to the east, has a total length of about 138 meters, with its circular posterior portion having a diameter of about 81 meters and a height of about 13.8 meters. The anterior rectangular portion, which is about 61 meters wide at the tip, spreads out in the shape of a [[shamisen]] [[plectrum]]. The posterior circular part is built in three stages and has remnants of [[breccia]] ''[[fukiishi]]'' and shards of trapezoidal-size cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. However, the tumulus has been much transformed from its original form, and its original scale is a matter of speculation. The anterior part was cultivated as a field, and now it is a park with [[sakura|cherry trees]]. The posterior portion has a bamboo grove and a cemetery on its north side. Residential complexes are located on the east and south sides of the burial mound. It is believed to have originally been a half-scale replica of the [[Hashihaka Kofun]] in [[Sakurai, Nara]] with which it is contemporary in age.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In 1988, an [[archaeological excavation]] confirmed a vertical pit-type stone [[burial chamber]] in the posterior porton of the tumulus, which was constructed by slabs of [[andesite]] which had been quarried in what is now northern [[Kagawa Prefecture]] or one of the islands in the [[Seto Inland Sea]]. The floor of the burial camber was covered with clay containing red pigment, and it is presumed that a split bamboo-shaped wooden coffin was placed inside. However, the burial chamber had been robbed during the [[Meiji period]] and most of the [[grave goods]] have been lost. The 1988 excavation did find fragments of a [[bronze mirror]], bronze and iron arrowheads, iron swords, spears and axes, and agricultural tools such as sickles and spades.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> File:Urama Chausuyama Kofun 07.jpg|Panoramic view File:Urama Chausuyama Kofun 03.jpg|Anterior Portion File:Urama Chausuyama Kofun 04.jpg|Posterior Portion File:Urama Chausuyama Kofun 06.jpg|Site of Burial Chamber </gallery>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Urama Chausuyama Kofun | native_name =浦間茶臼山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Urama Chausuyama Kofun air.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Aerial photo of Urama Chausuyama Kofun in 1980 | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Urama Chausuyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|42|36.2|N|134|4|1.3|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Higashi-ku, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid 3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|42|36.2|N|134|4|1.3|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Urama Chausuyama Kofun'''|浦間茶臼山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Urama neighborhood of [[Higashi-ku, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/172965|title=浦間茶臼山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is believed to have been built at the end of the 3rd century in the early Kofun period. It is one of the oldest large keyhole-shaped burial mounds in ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]].
==Overview== The Urama Chausuyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above. It is located on a small hill between the [[Yoshii River]] and the Sunagawa River flowing through eastern Okayama Prefecture. The tumulus, which is orientated to the east, has a total length of about 138 meters, with its circular posterior portion having a diameter of about 81 meters and a height of about 13.8 meters. The anterior rectangular portion, which is about 61 meters wide at the tip, spreads out in the shape of a [[shamisen]] [[plectrum]]. The posterior circular part is built in three stages and has remnants of [[breccia]] ''[[fukiishi]]'' and shards of trapezoidal-size cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. However, the tumulus has been much transformed from its original form, and its original scale is a matter of speculation. The anterior part was cultivated as a field, and now it is a park with [[sakura|cherry trees]]. The posterior portion has a bamboo grove and a cemetery on its north side. Residential complexes are located on the east and south sides of the burial mound. It is believed to have originally been a half-scale replica of the [[Hashihaka Kofun]] in [[Sakurai, Nara]] with which it is contemporary in age.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In 1988, an [[archaeological excavation]] confirmed a vertical pit-type stone [[burial chamber]] in the posterior porton of the tumulus, which was constructed by slabs of [[andesite]] which had been quarried in what is now northern [[Kagawa Prefecture]] or one of the islands in the [[Seto Inland Sea]]. The floor of the burial camber was covered with clay containing red pigment, and it is presumed that a split bamboo-shaped wooden coffin was placed inside. However, the burial chamber had been robbed during the [[Meiji period]] and most of the [[grave goods]] have been lost. The 1988 excavation did find fragments of a [[bronze mirror]], bronze and iron arrowheads, iron swords, spears and axes, and agricultural tools such as sickles and spades.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> File:Urama Chausuyama Kofun 07.jpg|Panoramic view File:Urama Chausuyama Kofun 03.jpg|Anterior Portion File:Urama Chausuyama Kofun 04.jpg|Posterior Portion File:Urama Chausuyama Kofun 06.jpg|Site of Burial Chamber </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Urama Chausuyama Kofun | native_name =浦間茶臼山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Urama Chausuyama Kofun air.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Aerial photo of Urama Chausuyama Kofun in 1980 | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Urama Chausuyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|42|36.2|N|134|4|1.3|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Higashi-ku, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid 3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|42|36.2|N|134|4|1.3|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Urama Chausuyama Kofun'''|浦間茶臼山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Urama neighborhood of [[Higashi-ku, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/172965|title=浦間茶臼山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is believed to have been built at the end of the 3rd century in the early Kofun period. It is one of the oldest large keyhole-shaped Kofuns in ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]].
==Overview== The Urama Chausuyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above. It is located on a small hill between the [[Yoshii River]] and the Sunagawa River flowing through eastern Okayama Prefecture. The tumulus, which is orientated to the east, has a total length of about 138 meters, with its circular posterior portion having a diameter of about 81 meters and a height of about 13.8 meters. The anterior rectangular portion, which is about 61 meters wide at the tip, spreads out in the shape of a [[shamisen]] [[plectrum]]. The posterior circular part is built in three stages and has remnants of [[breccia]] ''[[fukiishi]]'' and shards of trapezoidal-size cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. However, the tumulus has been much transformed from its original form, and its original scale is a matter of speculation. The anterior part was cultivated as a field, and now it is a park with [[sakura|cherry trees]]. The posterior portion has a bamboo grove and a cemetery on its north side. Residential complexes are located on the east and south sides of the Kofun. It is believed to have originally been a half-scale replica of the [[Hashihaka Kofun]] in [[Sakurai, Nara]] with which it is contemporary in age.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In 1988, an [[archaeological excavation]] confirmed a vertical pit-type stone [[burial chamber]] in the posterior porton of the tumulus, which was constructed by slabs of [[andesite]] which had been quarried in what is now northern [[Kagawa Prefecture]] or one of the islands in the [[Seto Inland Sea]]. The floor of the burial camber was covered with clay containing red pigment, and it is presumed that a split bamboo-shaped wooden coffin was placed inside. However, the burial chamber had been robbed during the [[Meiji period]] and most of the [[grave goods]] have been lost. The 1988 excavation did find fragments of a [[bronze mirror]], bronze and iron arrowheads, iron swords, spears and axes, and agricultural tools such as sickles and spades.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Urama Chausuyama Kofun | native_name =浦間茶臼山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Urama Chausuyama Kofun air.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Aerial photo of Urama Chausuyama Kofun in 1980 | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Urama Chausuyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|42|36.2|N|134|4|1.3|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Higashi-ku, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid 3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|42|36.2|N|134|4|1.3|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Urama Chausuyama Kofun'''|浦間茶臼山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Urama neighborhood of [[Higashi-ku, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/172965|title=浦間茶臼山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is believed to have been built at the end of the 3rd century in the early Kofun period. It is one of the oldest large keyhole-shaped Kofuns in ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]].
==Overview== The Urama Chausuyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above. It is located on a small hill between the [[Yoshii River]] and the Sunagawa River flowing through eastern Okayama Prefecture. The tumulus, which is orientated to the east, has a total length of about 138 meters, with its circular posterior portion having a diameter of about 81 meters and a height of about 13.8 meters. The anterior rectangular portion, which is about 61 meters wide at the tip, spreads out in the shape of a [[shamisen]] [[plectrum]]. The posterior circular part is built in three stages and has remnants of [[breccia]] ''[[fukiishi]]'' and shards of trapezoidal-size cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. However, the tumulus has been much transformed from its original form, and its original scale is a matter of speculation. The anterior part was cultivated as a field, and now it is a park with [[sakura|cherry trees]]. The posterior portion has a bamboo grove and a cemetery on its north side. Residential complexes are located on the east and south sides of the Kofun. It is believed to have originally been a half-scale replica of the [[Hashihaka Kofun]] in [[Sakurai, Nara]] with which it is contemporary in age.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In 1988, an [[archaeological excavation]] confirmed a vertical pit-type stone [[burial chamber]] in the posterior porton of the tumulus, which was constructed by slabs of [[andesite]] which had been quarried in what is now northern [[Kagawa Prefecture]] or one of the islands in the [[Seto Inland Sea]]. The floor of the burial camber was covered with clay containing red pigment, and it is presumed that a split bamboo-shaped wooden coffin was placed inside. However, the burial chamber had been robbed during the [[Meiji period]] and most of the [[grave goods]] have been lost. The 1988 excavation did find fragments of a [[bronze mirror]], bronze and iron arrowheads, iron swords, spears and axes, and agricultural tools such as sickles and spades.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Urama Chausuyama Kofun | native_name =浦間茶臼山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Urama Chausuyama Kofun air.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Aerial photo of Urama Chausuyama Kofun in 1980 | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Urama Chausuyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|42|36.2|N|134|4|1.3|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Higashi-ku, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid 3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|42|36.2|N|134|4|1.3|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Urama Chausuyama Kofun'''|浦間茶臼山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Urama neighborhood of [[Higashi-ku, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/172965|title=浦間茶臼山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is believed to have been built at the end of the 3rd century in the early Kofun period. It is one of the oldest large keyhole-shaped Kofuns in ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]].
==Overview== The Urama Chausuyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above. It is located on a small hill between the [[Yoshii River]] and the Sunagawa River flowing through eastern Okayama Prefecture. The tumulus, which is orientated to the east, has a total length of about 138 meters, with its circular posterior portion having a diameter of about 81 meters and a height of about 13.8 meters. The anterior rectangular portion, which is about 61 meters wide at the tip, spreads out in the shape of a [[shamisen]] [[plectrum]]. The posterior circular part is built in three stages and has remnants of [[breccia]] ''[[fukiishi]]'' and shards of trapezoidal-size cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. However, the tumulus has been much transformed from its original form, and its original scale is a matter of speculation. The anterior part was cultivated as a field, and now it is a park with [[sakura|cherry trees]]. The posterior portion has a bamboo grove and a cemetery on its north side. Residential complexes are located on the east and south sides of the Kofun. It is believed to have originally been a half-scale replica of the [[Hashihaka Kofun]] in [[Sakurai, Nara]] with which it is contemporary in age.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In 1988, an [[archaeological excavation]] confirmed a vertical pit-type stone [[burial chamber]] in the posterior porton of the tumulus, which was constructed by slabs of [[andesite]] which had been quarried in what is now northern [[Kagawa Prefecture]] or one of the islands in the [[Seto Inland Sea]]. The floor of the burial camber was covered with clay containing red pigment, and it is presumed that a split bamboo-shaped wooden coffin was placed inside. However, the burial chamber had been robbed during the [[Meiji period]] and most of the [[grave goods]] have been lost. The 1988 excavation did find fragments of a [[bronze mirror]], bronze and iron arrowheads, iron swords, spears and axes, and agricultural tools such as sickles and spades.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Urama Chausuyama Kofun | native_name =浦間茶臼山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Urama Chausuyama Kofun air.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Aerial photo of Urama Chausuyama Kofun in 1980 | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Urama Chausuyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|42|36.2|N|134|4|1.3|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Higashi-ku, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid 3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|42|36.2|N|134|4|1.3|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Urama Chausuyama Kofun'''|浦間茶臼山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Urama neighborhood of [[Higashi-ku, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/172965|title=浦間茶臼山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is believed to have been built at the end of the 3rd century in the early Kofun period. It is one of the oldest large keyhole-shaped Kofuns in ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]].
==Overview== The Urama Chausuyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above. It is located on a small hill between the [[Yoshii River]] and the Sunagawa River flowing through eastern Okayama Prefecture. The tumulus, which is orientated to the east, has a total length of about 138 meters, with its circular posterior portion having a diameter of about 81 meters and a height of about 13.8 meters. The anterior rectangular portion, which is about 61 meters wide at the tip, spreads out in the shape of a [[shamisen]] [[plectrum]]. The posterior circular part is built in three stages and has remnants of [[breccia]] ''[[fukiishi]]'' and shards of trapezoidal-size cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. However, the tumulus has been much transformed from its original form, and its original scale is a matter of speculation. The anterior part was cultivated as a field, and now it is a park with [[sakura|cherry trees]]. The posterior portion has a bamboo grove and a cemetery on its north side. Residential complexes are located on the east and south sides of the Kofun. It is believed to have originally been a half-scale replica of the [[Hashihaka Kofun]] in [[Sakurai, Nara]] with which it is contemporary in age.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In 1988, an [[archaeological excavation]] confirmed a vertical pit-type stone [[burial chamber]] in the posterior porton of the tumulus, which was constructed by slabs of [[andesite]] which had been quarried in what is now northern [[Kagawa Prefecture]] or one of the islands in the [[Seto Inland Sea]]. The floor of the burial camber was covered with clay containing red pigment, and it is presumed that a split bamboo-shaped wooden coffin was placed inside. However, the burial chamber had been robbed during the [[Meiji period]] and most of the [[grave goods]] have been lost. The 1988 excavation did find fragments of a [[bronze mirror]], bronze and iron arrowheads, iron swords, spears and axes, and agricultural tools such as sickles and spades.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> File:Urama Chausuyama Kofun 07.jpg|Panoramic view File:Urama Chausuyama Kofun 03.jpg|Anterior Portion File:Urama Chausuyama Kofun 04.jpg|Posterior Portion File:Urama Chausuyama Kofun 06.jpg|Site of Burial Chamber </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Urama Chausuyama Kofun | native_name =浦間茶臼山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Urama Chausuyama Kofun air.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Aerial photo of Urama Chausuyama Kofun in 1980 | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Urama Chausuyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|42|36.2|N|134|4|1.3|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Higashi-ku, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid 3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|42|36.2|N|134|4|1.3|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Urama Chausuyama Kofun'''|浦間茶臼山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Urama neighborhood of [[Higashi-ku, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/172965|title=浦間茶臼山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is believed to have been built at the end of the 3rd century in the early Kofun period. It is one of the oldest large keyhole-shaped Kofuns in ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]].
==Overview== The Urama Chausuyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above. It is located on a small hill between the [[Yoshii River]] and the Sunagawa River flowing through eastern Okayama Prefecture. The tumulus, which is orientated to the east, has a total length of about 138 meters, with its circular posterior portion having a diameter of about 81 meters and a height of about 13.8 meters. The anterior rectangular portion, which is about 61 meters wide at the tip, spreads out in the shape of a [[shamisen]] [[plectrum]]. The posterior circular part is built in three stages and has remnants of [[breccia]] ''[[fukiishi]]'' and shards of trapezoidal-size cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. However, the tumulus has been much transformed from its original form, and its original scale is a matter of speculation. The anterior part was cultivated as a field, and now it is a park with [[sakura|cherry trees]]. The posterior portion has a bamboo grove and a cemetery on its north side. Residential complexes are located on the east and south sides of the Kofun. It is believed to have originally been a half-scale replica of the [[Hashihaka Kofun]] in [[Sakurai, Nara]] with which it is contemporary in age.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In 1988, an [[archaeological excavation]] confirmed a vertical pit-type stone [[burial chamber]] in the posterior porton of the tumulus, which was constructed by slabs of [[andesite]] which had been quarried in what is now northern [[Kagawa Prefecture]] or one of the islands in the [[Seto Inland Sea]]. The floor of the burial camber was covered with clay containing red pigment, and it is presumed that a split bamboo-shaped wooden coffin was placed inside. However, the burial chamber had been robbed during the [[Meiji period]] and most of the [[grave goods]] have been lost. The 1988 excavation did find fragments of a [[bronze mirror]], bronze and iron arrowheads, iron swords, spears and axes, and agricultural tools such as sickles and spades.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> File:Urama Chausuyama Kofun 07.jpg|Panoramic view File:Urama Chausuyama Kofun 03.jpg|Anterior Portion File:Urama Chausuyama Kofun 04.jpg|Posterior Portion File:Urama Chausuyama Kofun 06.jpg|Site of Burial Chamber </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Urama Chausuyama Kofun | native_name =浦間茶臼山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Urama Chausuyama Kofun air.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Aerial photo of Urama Chausuyama Kofun in 1980 | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Urama Chausuyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|42|36.2|N|134|4|1.3|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Higashi-ku, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid 3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|42|36.2|N|134|4|1.3|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Urama Chausuyama Kofun'''|浦間茶臼山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Urama neighborhood of [[Higashi-ku, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/172965|title=浦間茶臼山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is believed to have been built at the end of the 3rd century in the early Kofun period. It is one of the oldest large keyhole-shaped Kofuns in ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]].
==Overview== The Urama Chausuyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above. It is located on a small hill between the [[Yoshii River]] and the Sunagawa River flowing through eastern Okayama Prefecture. The tumulus, which is orientated to the east, has a total length of about 138 meters, with its circular posterior portion having a diameter of about 81 meters and a height of about 13.8 meters. The anterior rectangular portion, which is about 61 meters wide at the tip, spreads out in the shape of a [[shamisen]] [[plectrum]]. The posterior circular part is built in three stages and has remnants of [[breccia]] ''[[fukiishi]]'' and shards of trapezoidal-size cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. However, the tumulus has been much transformed from its original form, and its original scale is a matter of speculation. The anterior part was cultivated as a field, and now it is a park with [[sakura|cherry trees]]. The posterior portion has a bamboo grove and a cemetery on its north side. Residential complexes are located on the east and south sides of the Kofun. It is believed to have originally been a half-scale replica of the [[Hashihaka Kofun]] in [[Sakurai, Nara]] with which it is contemporary in age.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In 1988, an [[archaeological excavation]] confirmed a vertical pit-type stone [[burial chamber]] in the posterior porton of the tumulus, which was constructed by slabs of [[andesite]] which had been quarried in what is now northern [[Kagawa Prefecture]] or one of the islands in the [[Seto Inland Sea]]. The floor of the burial camber was covered with clay containing red pigment, and it is presumed that a split bamboo-shaped wooden coffin was placed inside. However, the burial chamber had been robbed during the [[Meiji period]] and most of the [[grave goods]] have been lost. The 1988 excavation did find fragments of a [[bronze mirror]], bronze and iron arrowheads, iron swords, spears and axes, and agricultural tools such as sickles and spades.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Urama Chausuyama Kofun | native_name =浦間茶臼山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Urama Chausuyama Kofun air.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Aerial photo of Urama Chausuyama Kofun in 1980 | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Urama Chausuyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|42|36.2|N|134|4|1.3|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Higashi-ku, Okayama|Higashi-ku, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid 3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|42|36.2|N|134|4|1.3|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Urama Chausuyama Kofun'''|浦間茶臼山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Urama neighborhood of [[:en:Higashi-ku, Okayama|Higashi-ku, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/172965|title=浦間茶臼山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is believed to have been built at the end of the 3rd century in the early Kofun period. It is one of the oldest large keyhole-shaped Kofuns in ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]].
==Overview== The Urama Chausuyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above. It is located on a small hill between the [[Yoshii River]] and the Sunagawa River flowing through eastern Okayama Prefecture. The tumulus, which is orientated to the east, has a total length of about 138 meters, with its circular posterior portion having a diameter of about 81 meters and a height of about 13.8 meters. The anterior rectangular portion, which is about 61 meters wide at the tip, spreads out in the shape of a [[shamisen]] [[plectrum]]. The posterior circular part is built in three stages and has remnants of [[:en:Breccia|Breccia]] ''[[fukiishi]]'' and shards of trapezoidal-size cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]''. However, the tumulus has been much transformed from its original form, and its original scale is a matter of speculation. The anterior part was cultivated as a field, and now it is a park with [[sakura|cherry trees]]. The posterior portion has a bamboo grove and a cemetery on its north side. Residential complexes are located on the east and south sides of the Kofun. It is believed to have originally been a half-scale replica of the [[:en:Hashihaka Kofun|Hashihaka Kofun]] in [[Sakurai, Nara]] with which it is contemporary in age.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In 1988, an [[archaeological excavation]] confirmed a vertical pit-type stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] in the posterior porton of the tumulus, which was constructed by slabs of [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]] which had been quarried in what is now northern [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]] or one of the islands in the [[Seto Inland Sea]]. The floor of the burial camber was covered with clay containing red pigment, and it is presumed that a split bamboo-shaped wooden coffin was placed inside. However, the burial chamber had been robbed during the [[Meiji period]] and most of the [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] have been lost. The 1988 excavation did find fragments of a [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]], bronze and iron arrowheads, iron swords, spears and axes, and agricultural tools such as sickles and spades.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> File:Urama Chausuyama Kofun 07.jpg|Panoramic view File:Urama Chausuyama Kofun 03.jpg|Anterior Portion File:Urama Chausuyama Kofun 04.jpg|Posterior Portion File:Urama Chausuyama Kofun 06.jpg|Site of Burial Chamber </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]][[Category:Urama Chausuyama Kofun}} *https://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/01.html Okayama City home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.okayama.jp/site/kodai/632635.html Okayama Prefecture home page]{{in lang|ja}}

The floor of the burial camber was covered with clay containing red pigment, and it is presumed that a split bamboo-shaped wooden coffin was placed inside. However, the burial chamber had been robbed during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] and most of the [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] have been lost. The 1988 excavation did find fragments of a [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]], bronze and iron arrowheads, iron swords, spears and axes, and agricultural tools such as sickles and spades.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{nihongo|'''Urama Chausuyama Kofun'''|浦間茶臼山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Urama neighborhood of [[:en:Higashi-ku, Okayama|Higashi-ku, Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/172965|title=浦間茶臼山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is believed to have been built at the end of the 3rd century in the early Kofun period. It is one of the oldest large keyhole-shaped Kofuns in ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]].
==Overview== The Urama Chausuyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above. It is located on a small hill between the [[Yoshii River]] and the Sunagawa River flowing through eastern Okayama Prefecture. The tumulus, which is orientated to the east, has a total length of about 138 meters, with its circular posterior portion having a diameter of about 81 meters and a height of about 13.8 meters. The anterior rectangular portion, which is about 61 meters wide at the tip, spreads out in the shape of a [[shamisen]] [[:en:plectrum|plectrum]]. The posterior circular part is built in three stages and has remnants of [[:en:Breccia|Breccia]] ''[[fukiishi]]'' and shards of trapezoidal-size cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]''. However, the tumulus has been much transformed from its original form, and its original scale is a matter of speculation. The anterior part was cultivated as a field, and now it is a park with [[sakura|cherry trees]]. The posterior portion has a bamboo grove and a cemetery on its north side. Residential complexes are located on the east and south sides of the Kofun. It is believed to have originally been a half-scale replica of the [[:en:Hashihaka Kofun|Hashihaka Kofun]] in [[Sakurai, Nara]] with which it is contemporary in age.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In 1988, an [[archaeological excavation]] confirmed a vertical pit-type stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] in the posterior porton of the tumulus, which was constructed by slabs of [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]] which had been quarried in what is now northern [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]] or one of the islands in the [[Seto Inland Sea]]. The floor of the burial camber was covered with clay containing red pigment, and it is presumed that a split bamboo-shaped wooden coffin was placed inside. However, the burial chamber had been robbed during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] and most of the [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] have been lost. The 1988 excavation did find fragments of a [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]], bronze and iron arrowheads, iron swords, spears and axes, and agricultural tools such as sickles and spades.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Urama Chausuyama Kofun | native_name =浦間茶臼山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Urama Chausuyama Kofun air.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Aerial photo of Urama Chausuyama Kofun in 1980 | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Urama Chausuyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|42|36.2|N|134|4|1.3|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Higashi-ku, Okayama|Higashi-ku, Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid 3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|42|36.2|N|134|4|1.3|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Urama Chausuyama Kofun'''|浦間茶臼山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Urama neighborhood of [[:en:Higashi-ku, Okayama|Higashi-ku, Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/172965|title=浦間茶臼山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is believed to have been built at the end of the 3rd century in the early Kofun period. It is one of the oldest large keyhole-shaped Kofuns in ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]].
==Overview== The Urama Chausuyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above. It is located on a small hill between the [[:en:Yoshii River|Yoshii River]] and the Sunagawa River flowing through eastern Okayama Prefecture. The tumulus, which is orientated to the east, has a total length of about 138 meters, with its circular posterior portion having a diameter of about 81 meters and a height of about 13.8 meters. The anterior rectangular portion, which is about 61 meters wide at the tip, spreads out in the shape of a [[shamisen]] [[:en:plectrum|plectrum]]. The posterior circular part is built in three stages and has remnants of [[:en:Breccia|Breccia]] ''[[fukiishi]]'' and shards of trapezoidal-size cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]''. However, the tumulus has been much transformed from its original form, and its original scale is a matter of speculation. The anterior part was cultivated as a field, and now it is a park with [[:en:Sakura|cherry trees]]. The posterior portion has a bamboo grove and a cemetery on its north side. Residential complexes are located on the east and south sides of the Kofun. It is believed to have originally been a half-scale replica of the [[:en:Hashihaka Kofun|Hashihaka Kofun]] in [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai, Nara]] with which it is contemporary in age.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In 1988, an [[archaeological excavation]] confirmed a vertical pit-type stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] in the posterior porton of the tumulus, which was constructed by slabs of [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]] which had been quarried in what is now northern [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]] or one of the islands in the [[Seto Inland Sea]]. The floor of the burial camber was covered with clay containing red pigment, and it is presumed that a split bamboo-shaped wooden coffin was placed inside. However, the burial chamber had been robbed during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] and most of the [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] have been lost. The 1988 excavation did find fragments of a [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]], bronze and iron arrowheads, iron swords, spears and axes, and agricultural tools such as sickles and spades.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> File:Urama Chausuyama Kofun 07.jpg|Panoramic view File:Urama Chausuyama Kofun 03.jpg|Anterior Portion File:Urama Chausuyama Kofun 04.jpg|Posterior Portion File:Urama Chausuyama Kofun 06.jpg|Site of Burial Chamber </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]
